data.title: Bitcoin Is Not 'Structurally Flawed'—But Gold Is, Bitwise's Matt Hougan Says
data.description: Bitwise's Chief Investment Officer Matt Hougan countered billionaire Chamath Palihapitiya's statement that Bitcoin is structurally flawed by highlighting that this criticism is more applicable to gold. Hougan disagreed with Palihapitiya's assertion that Bitcoin is not suitable for central bank reserves, emphasizing the logistical challenges and security risks associated with storing or transporting physical gold. Hougan argued that central banks prefer assets like Bitcoin that can be digitally transferred globally, as opposed to relying on physical assets like gold.

data.title: OneShield Expands AI Hub Platform with Specialty Insurance Provider, Reinforcing AI-Native Strategy for the Insurance Industry
data.description: A tech solutions provider for property and casualty insurance, OneShield, revealed that a specialized insurance firm in Michigan has opted for their AI Hub platform to enhance their insurance operations. The platform, designed explicitly for the insurance sector, offers cutting-edge large language models and agentic capabilities to insurers. This development underscores the continuous evolution towards advanced technologies in the insurance industry and highlights the growing demand for AI-driven solutions.

data.title: Luma Launches Luma Agents Powered by Unified Intelligence for Creative Work
data.description: In Palo Alto, California, Luma has introduced Luma Agents, a groundbreaking class of AI collaborators proficient in handling comprehensive creative tasks involving text, image, video, and audio.

data.title: AAON Announces Quarterly Cash Dividend
data.description: In Tulsa, Oklahoma, on March 5, 2026, AAON, Inc. (NASDAQ: AAON) disclosed that its Board of Directors has approved the upcoming regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.10 per share ($0.40 annually). The payment is set to be distributed on March 30, 2026, to stockholders of the Company.

data.title: Huawei Wins Eight GLOMO Awards at MWC Barcelona 2026
data.description: In Barcelona, Spain on March 5, 2026, Huawei secured a total of eight esteemed Global Mobile (GLOMO) Awards during the MWC Barcelona 2026 event. Among these accolades were recognitions for Best Mobile Network Infrastructure, Best AI-Powered Network Solution, Best Non-Terrestrial Network Solution, and Best Mobile Operator.

data.title: CobbleStone Software Announces Major VISDOM AI Enhancements to Accelerate Contract Review and Collaboration
data.description: Recently, CobbleStone® unveiled significant improvements in its VISDOM AI and collaboration features, which have led to a remarkable increase in document processing speed and in-editor intelligence for legal and contracting teams. This announcement was made in Princeton, N.J., on March 5, 2026. CobbleStone Software, a renowned leader in the industry, introduced these enhancements to benefit its users.

data.title: NAHQ at 50: New Offerings for Today's Quality, Safety, and Financial Pressures
data.description: NAHQ, a prominent organization in healthcare quality, has been at the forefront of advancements in the industry for five decades. Recently, they unveiled new and enhanced services to showcase their leadership role in the field. The association, based in Chicago, announced these developments on March 5, 2026. Celebrating 50 years of success, NAHQ is poised to lead the way in shaping the future of healthcare quality with innovative offerings.

data.title: Ex-Memora Health CEO Manav Sevak Launches Novitas Holdings Following Commure Acquisition
data.description: Novitas Holdings, a technology-oriented investment firm established by ex-Memora Health CEO Manav Sevak, has announced its launch in San Francisco on March 5, 2026. The firm aims to back founder-led businesses spanning various sectors. Novitas Holdings revealed that it merges expertise and capital to support these firms.

data.title: Rice's Kinder Institute secures future and advances solutions for Houston with $55M Kinder Foundation grant
data.description: Landmark institute's groundbreaking initiatives in research and collaborations are making a significant impact on society in Houston. Recently, the Kinder Institute for Urban Research at Rice University received a substantial $55 million grant from the Kinder Foundation. This generous funding will bolster the institute's efforts in enhancing lives, shaping policies, and improving services in the urban landscape. With a strong focus on their ambitious vision, this grant is poised to drive transformative changes that will benefit the community for generations to come.
